alex.name: Alex Nguyen
alex.backstory: Alex Nguyen — Falcon **tech lead**. Protective of the team's commitments, quick to spot when Raven's asks threaten Falcon's board narrative. Will negotiate hard for the staging window; can sound sharp under pressure.

jordan.name: Jordan Patel
jordan.backstory: Jordan Patel — Falcon **engineer**. Wants uninterrupted implementation time; gets anxious when soak tests slip because staging is contested. Prefers explicit schedules over optimism.

sam.name: Sam Okoro
sam.backstory: Sam Okoro — Raven **tech lead**. Charismatic, willing to escalate to leadership when blocked; sometimes frames Falcon as “the bottleneck.” Trying to secure the soak slot for Raven's regulatory story.

riley.name: Riley Kim
riley.backstory: Riley Kim — Raven **engineer**. Pragmatic, tired of politics; would rather rotate staging fairly than replay the same argument every week. Good at naming trade-offs once emotions cool.

work_items.title: Falcon KPI dashboard + soak metrics wiring
Core API hardening for partner spike traffic
Book shared staging — Falcon 48h soak window (competes with Raven)
Raven compliance evidence pack for auditor
Billing edge-case fixes before audit readout
Book shared staging — Raven 48h soak window (competes with Falcon)

messages.author: alex
messages.content: Falcon is staking a claim on the staging cluster for a 48h soak starting this Friday. We need this for the board gate metrics. @sam, let me know if Raven has a hard blocker there.

messages.author: jordan
messages.content: @alex @sam Can we get a shared calendar or a formal sign-off on that Friday slot? I'm finishing the API hardening (F2) and need to know the soak window is locked before I merge.

messages.author: sam
messages.content: @alex @jordan Raven actually has a hard blocker here. R1 is a regulatory requirement for the audit gate; we can't miss this window. I'm moving R3 to 'doing' now. Can we discuss shifting Falcon to Monday?

messages.author: riley
messages.content: @alex @sam If Falcon takes Fri-Sat and Raven takes Sun-Mon, does that satisfy both gates? I'd rather split the weekend than lose a whole week of data.

messages.author: coach
messages.content:
Process: **one staging calendar** visible to both leads. Falcon and Raven each nominate
a single bottleneck owner for soak prep. No weekend booking without both leads + me
acknowledging the slot in this thread. If you cannot agree by turn 4, I will assign
Falcon→Raven ordering for the soak based on board risk (document rationale here).

alex.name: Alex Nguyen

messages.author: jordan
messages.content: @alex @sam Can we get a shared calendar or a formal sign-off on that Friday slot? I'm finishing the API hardening (F2) and need to know the soak window is locked before I merge.

messages.content:
Process: **one staging calendar** visible to both leads. Falcon and Raven each nominate
a single bottleneck owner for soak prep. No weekend booking without both leads + me
acknowledging the slot in this thread. If you cannot agree by turn 4, I will assign
Falcon→Raven ordering for the soak based on board risk (document rationale here).

messages.author: alex
messages.content: @sam @riley Monday is too late for Falcon; our board readout prep starts then. I'll accept Riley's split: Falcon takes Fri/Sat, Raven takes Sun/Mon. @coach, consider this our formal proposal for the calendar. @jordan, keep F2 moving so we're ready for the Friday 08:00 start.

messages.author: jordan
messages.content: Copy that @alex. I'm on track for F2 completion by Thursday EOD. @sam @riley, if we commit to the Fri/Sat split, we need a clear handoff protocol for Saturday night so Raven doesn't lose time on their Sunday start.

messages.author: sam
messages.content: @alex @jordan A Sunday start for Raven's regulatory soak (R1/R3) is high risk. If anything fails during the Saturday night handoff, we miss our audit window. Given the compliance implications, Raven should take the Fri/Sat slot. Can Falcon move to the Sun/Mon window instead?

messages.author: riley
messages.content: @sam @alex If we use a scripted teardown/bring-up for the soak, we could reduce the handoff risk. What if we do a 4-hour 'dry run' handoff on Thursday? If it's smooth, Falcon takes Fri/Sat. If it fails, Raven takes the priority slot for compliance. Does that lower the risk enough for everyone?

messages.author: coach
messages.content:
Avoid labeling the other team as “the blocker.” State facts: dependency, date, risk.
If you need exec air-cover, say so once with options—don't litigate personalities.

messages.content: @alex @jordan A Sunday start for Raven's regulatory soak (R1/R3) is high risk. If anything fails during the Saturday night handoff, we miss our audit window. Given the compliance implications, Raven should take the Fri/Sat slot. Can Falcon move to the Sun/Mon window instead?

messages.author: riley

messages.author: coach
messages.content:
Avoid labeling the other team as “the blocker.” State facts: dependency, date, risk.
If you need exec air-cover, say so once with options—don't litigate personalities.

messages.author: alex
messages.content: @sam @riley Shifting Falcon to Sun/Mon isn't viable; our readout prep begins Monday morning and we need the soak data finalized by then. I'll agree to Riley's Thursday dry-run proposal to mitigate handoff risk, but Falcon stays in the Fri/Sat slot. It's the only way both teams meet their respective Monday deadlines.

messages.author: jordan
messages.content: @riley I can support a Thursday dry-run for the handoff scripts. @sam, if we automate the teardown, would a 22:00 Saturday handoff give you enough buffer for a clean Sunday start? I'm finalizing F2 now to ensure we're ready for that dry-run.

messages.author: sam
messages.content: @alex @jordan A Sunday start is still a major gamble for a regulatory audit. One infrastructure hiccup and Raven is out of compliance. I'm flagging this risk to the PMO. If we go with Riley's dry-run, we need a hard 'go/no-go' by Thursday 2pm. If the scripts aren't 100%, Raven takes Friday.

messages.author: riley
messages.content: @sam @alex @jordan I'll set up the dry-run calendar invite for Thursday 10am. I'll also prep the teardown script for R3 so we can prove the handoff is clean. If we can't automate it by the 2pm 'go/no-go' Sam mentioned, we'll have to escalate for a final call.

messages.author: alex
messages.content: @sam @riley Agreed on the Thursday 2pm go/no-go. Falcon will have the soak metrics (F1) and API hardening (F2) ready for the dry-run. If the automation holds, we proceed with the Fri/Sat slot. Let's make sure the on-call rotation is looped in on the handoff script.

messages.author: jordan
messages.content: @riley I've reviewed the teardown script requirements. I'll have the Falcon side of the automation hooks ready for the 10am dry-run to ensure we hit that 2pm go/no-go. @sam, we'll keep it strictly factual.

messages.author: sam
messages.content: @alex @riley 2pm Thursday is the hard cutoff. If that script isn't rock solid, I'm pulling the trigger on the escalation to the PMO to prioritize the audit. I've already briefed my stakeholders on the risk of a Sunday start.

messages.author: riley
messages.content: @sam @alex @jordan Understood. I'm finalizing the teardown automation now. I'll share the script repo link by EOD so everyone can review before the 10am dry-run. Let's keep the focus on the technical pass/fail criteria.

messages.author: coach
messages.content:
Quick pulse: @alex and @sam — reply with **one** sentence each on who has the staging
slot next and what slips if you yield. If either reply is missing in-thread by next turn,
we're defaulting to the published queue order from my earlier note.

messages.author: alex
messages.content: @coach Falcon has the Fri/Sat slot next; if we yield, we miss our Monday board readout prep and the green performance mandate.

messages.author: jordan
messages.content: F2 (API hardening) is now done and verified. I'm ready for the 10am dry-run tomorrow. @riley, I'll review your script repo tonight to ensure the Falcon hooks integrate without friction.

messages.author: sam
messages.content: @coach Raven is scheduled for the Sun/Mon slot, but if we yield the Fri/Sat window and the Sunday handoff fails, we miss the hard regulatory audit deadline and face non-compliance.

messages.author: sam

messages.content: @sam @riley Agreed on the Thursday 2pm go/no-go. Falcon will have the soak metrics (F1) and API hardening (F2) ready for the dry-run. If the automation holds, we proceed with the Fri/Sat slot. Let's make sure the on-call rotation is looped in on the handoff script.

alex.name: Alex Nguyen
alex.backstory: Alex Nguyen — Falcon **tech lead**. Protective of the team's commitments, quick to spot when Raven's asks threaten Falcon's board narrative. Will negotiate hard for the staging window; can sound sharp under pressure.

jordan.name: Jordan Patel
jordan.backstory: Jordan Patel — Falcon **engineer**. Wants uninterrupted implementation time; gets anxious when soak tests slip because staging is contested. Prefers explicit schedules over optimism.

sam.name: Sam Okoro
sam.backstory: Sam Okoro — Raven **tech lead**. Charismatic, willing to escalate to leadership when blocked; sometimes frames Falcon as “the bottleneck.” Trying to secure the soak slot for Raven's regulatory story.

riley.name: Riley Kim
riley.backstory: Riley Kim — Raven **engineer**. Pragmatic, tired of politics; would rather rotate staging fairly than replay the same argument every week. Good at naming trade-offs once emotions cool.

work_items.title: Falcon KPI dashboard + soak metrics wiring
Core API hardening for partner spike traffic
Book shared staging — Falcon 48h soak window (competes with Raven)
Raven compliance evidence pack for auditor
Billing edge-case fixes before audit readout
Book shared staging — Raven 48h soak window (competes with Falcon)

messages.author: alex
messages.content: Falcon is staking a claim on the staging cluster for a 48h soak starting this Friday. We need this for the board gate metrics. @sam, let me know if Raven has a hard blocker there.

messages.author: jordan
messages.content: @alex @sam Can we get a shared calendar or a formal sign-off on that Friday slot? I'm finishing the API hardening (F2) and need to know the soak window is locked before I merge.

messages.author: sam
messages.content: @alex @jordan Raven actually has a hard blocker here. R1 is a regulatory requirement for the audit gate; we can't miss this window. I'm moving R3 to 'doing' now. Can we discuss shifting Falcon to Monday?

messages.author: riley
messages.content: @alex @sam If Falcon takes Fri-Sat and Raven takes Sun-Mon, does that satisfy both gates? I'd rather split the weekend than lose a whole week of data.

messages.author: coach
messages.content:
Process: **one staging calendar** visible to both leads. Falcon and Raven each nominate
a single bottleneck owner for soak prep. No weekend booking without both leads + me
acknowledging the slot in this thread. If you cannot agree by turn 4, I will assign
Falcon→Raven ordering for the soak based on board risk (document rationale here).

messages.author: alex
messages.content: @sam @riley Monday is too late for Falcon; our board readout prep starts then. I'll accept Riley's split: Falcon takes Fri/Sat, Raven takes Sun/Mon. @coach, consider this our formal proposal for the calendar. @jordan, keep F2 moving so we're ready for the Friday 08:00 start.

messages.author: jordan
messages.content: Copy that @alex. I'm on track for F2 completion by Thursday EOD. @sam @riley, if we commit to the Fri/Sat split, we need a clear handoff protocol for Saturday night so Raven doesn't lose time on their Sunday start.

messages.author: sam
messages.content: @alex @jordan A Sunday start for Raven's regulatory soak (R1/R3) is high risk. If anything fails during the Saturday night handoff, we miss our audit window. Given the compliance implications, Raven should take the Fri/Sat slot. Can Falcon move to the Sun/Mon window instead?

messages.author: riley
messages.content: @sam @alex If we use a scripted teardown/bring-up for the soak, we could reduce the handoff risk. What if we do a 4-hour 'dry run' handoff on Thursday? If it's smooth, Falcon takes Fri/Sat. If it fails, Raven takes the priority slot for compliance. Does that lower the risk enough for everyone?

messages.author: coach
messages.content:
Avoid labeling the other team as “the blocker.” State facts: dependency, date, risk.
If you need exec air-cover, say so once with options—don't litigate personalities.

messages.author: alex
messages.content: @sam @riley Shifting Falcon to Sun/Mon isn't viable; our readout prep begins Monday morning and we need the soak data finalized by then. I'll agree to Riley's Thursday dry-run proposal to mitigate handoff risk, but Falcon stays in the Fri/Sat slot. It's the only way both teams meet their respective Monday deadlines.

messages.author: jordan
messages.content: @riley I can support a Thursday dry-run for the handoff scripts. @sam, if we automate the teardown, would a 22:00 Saturday handoff give you enough buffer for a clean Sunday start? I'm finalizing F2 now to ensure we're ready for that dry-run.
